{"society_power": 6.0, "state_power": 6.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Parliamentary constitutionalism amid reaction to French Revolution", "reasoning": "Britain had an effective fiscal-military state with a robust tax and naval apparatus and functioning parliamentary institutions (moderate-to-strong state), while a vibrant press, associational life, and reform movements gave society real voice\u2014though wartime repression like the 1794-95 suspension of habeas corpus and the Gagging Acts constrained mobilization, keeping society moderate rather than dominant."}
